page: Amazon Web Services r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz, configured with 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, with the stress-ng multi-core test and Passmark CPU score falling into the middle 50% tier. However, single-core capabilities are relatively weak, as shown by bottom 25% scores in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. Memory bandwidth remains stable, measuring 50.08 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 35.76 GB/sec for large block write operations.

The primary tradeoff of the r5.2xlarge is its modest single-threaded compute performance and lack of local storage, which limits its suitability for intensive write-heavy local disk operations or raw single-core processing tasks. Conversely, its 64.0 GB memory capacity and average database operation benchmarks make it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-speed local storage. It is suited for database operations, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance is designed for memory-intensive applications. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, alongside weaker single-core and decompression performance. The instance represents a balanced option for memory-bound workloads where massive local storage is not required. It is best suited for database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for database operations and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-CPU ratio for database operations and caching workloads without local storage requirements.

64.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Average performance in database operations and multi-core benchmarks
Suited for Redis caching, database hosting, and static web serving
